mechanic.title: Pull-only crates with blocked portal fallback
mechanic.description: A compact Sokoban-like mechanic set where crates can only be pulled, portals are paired, and blocked portal exits cause the entrance portal to be pushed.

win.description: The player reaches a designated goal cell.

reachability_macro.notes: Portal movement is spatially nonlocal, so a future checker must include harmless portal edges before enabling reachability compression.

puzzlescript_next.notes: Exporter should keep rule, event, and knowledge ids as comments.

knowledge.statement: 玩家可以把身后相邻的单个箱子向自己的移动方向拉动。

evidence.notes: Declared by the core pull rule; needs witness verification.

knowledge.statement: 玩家不能推动前方的箱子。

counterfactual.description: Compare against a variant where crates are pushable.

knowledge.statement: 玩家可以通过拉开堵路箱子制造通路。

source.notes: Tactic-level knowledge expected to be verified through levels.

informal_semantics.notes: Non-formal design hypothesis; not used by evaluator proof.

knowledge.statement: 玩家进入出口未堵塞的传送门时会被传送到配对传送门出口。
传送门出口被堵住时，玩家不会正常传送。

counterfactual.description: The blocked-exit behavior should be compared to ordinary teleport behavior.

knowledge.statement: 传送门出口被堵住且入口传送门前方可移动时，进入入口会改为推动入口传送门。
如果传送门出口被堵住且入口传送门也推不动，那么进入传送门的动作失败。

counterfactual.description: Compare against a variant where blocked portals are always pushable.

knowledge.statement: 玩家可以故意把箱子拉到传送门出口，从而触发堵塞出口行为。

source.notes: This should become trace-mined once the solver can identify causal blocking.

informal_semantics.notes: Non-formal design hypothesis; not used by evaluator proof.

knowledge.statement: 玩家可以利用堵塞出口的 fallback 推动入口传送门，从而改变可达区域。

source.notes: Strategy-level knowledge; should be backed by necessary levels.

informal_semantics.notes: Non-formal design hypothesis; not used by evaluator proof.

levels.title: First Pull
levels.design_notes: Minimal pull witness. Solver should verify whether the current win condition and layout produce the intended crate-on-goal result.

levels.title: No Push
levels.design_notes: The first visible attempt fails because the crate cannot be pushed. A verified version should require the player to route around and pull.

levels.title: Open The Hall
levels.design_notes: Intended to show that pulling a crate out of a choke point can create a path. The exact intended trace should be generated once runtime exists.

levels.title: Clean Portal
levels.design_notes: Establish ordinary portal behavior before blocked exits are introduced.

levels.title: Blocked Exit
levels.design_notes: The crate blocks portal B's exit, so entering A should push A rather than teleporting the player.

levels.title: Build The Block
levels.design_notes: Intended application: pull the crate onto the paired portal exit, then use the blocked-exit fallback to move the entrance portal.

levels.title: Portal As Key
levels.design_notes: The moved portal should become a spatial key that opens the upper route. Solver must reject this candidate if the goal is reachable without the fallback push.

levels.title: Jammed Portal
levels.design_notes: The paired exit is blocked by a crate and the entrance portal cannot move because a wall is directly behind it.

levels.title: Doorway Portal
levels.design_notes: The portal itself blocks the corridor. Repeated blocked-exit fallback pushes move it out of the way so the player can stand on the goal.

levels.title: Build And Open
levels.design_notes: First pull the crate into the paired portal's exit, then return to the entrance and push the portal along the corridor until the goal opens.

levels.title: Long Doorway
levels.design_notes: A longer corridor variant of Doorway Portal. The player must push the entrance portal multiple times before stepping onto the goal.

levels.title: Push Into Jam
levels.design_notes: The first blocked entry pushes A right. The second blocked entry cannot push A because the next cell is a wall.

levels.title: Longer Pull
levels.design_notes: A longer version of the first pull witness, used to make the pull motion feel deliberate rather than accidental.

levels.title: Hard Stop
levels.design_notes: A compact negative witness for the no-push rule.

levels.title: Sealed Shuttle
levels.design_notes: The goal chamber is sealed from the start area, so ordinary teleport is the only way to reach it.

levels.title: Vertical Doorway
levels.design_notes: A vertical version of the doorway idea. Blocked portal entries push A downward until the goal cell opens.

levels.title: Vertical Jam
levels.design_notes: The paired exit is blocked by a crate, but A cannot move because B is directly in its push destination.

levels.title: Very Long Doorway
levels.design_notes: A longer corridor that requires repeated fallback pushes before the player can walk to the goal.

levels.title: Three Push Jam
levels.design_notes: The entrance portal can move several times before reaching a wall and demonstrating the fallback failure condition.

levels.title: Wide Build
levels.design_notes: A wider version of Build The Block. The extra space gives the player room to see that the crate must become the portal exit blocker.
